texto: We had so many dreams as children. Where do they go when we grow? Are they swallowed up by the mundane things of everyday life? Or do we lose them, leave them behind us in the dust, for new children to find and take up?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Helen Hollick que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. Ao questionar “We had so many dreams as children. Where do they go when we grow? Are they swallowed up by the mu…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre motivacao, dreams, growing-up, com foco em growing-up e helen-hollick.

texto: Two people can only live as one when each is prepared to give and receive trust and understanding. Above that lies respect. Without respect for how the other feels, no marriage is worthwhile.
explicacao: Citação de Helen Hollick sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “Two people can only live as one when each is prepared to give and receive trust and understanding…” articula autoestima, love, marriage em registro de autoestima, com relevância para contextos como casamento e marriage.

texto: It never leaves, not with someone we love. Their presence burns too vivid in our memories. Happen that is as it should be, for otherwise we would too easy forget.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Helen Hollick sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “It never leaves, not with someone we love. Their presence burns too vivid in our memories. Happen…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amor, love, memories, útil em momentos de memories.

texto: Change has to come for life to struggle forward.
explicacao: Citação de Helen Hollick sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “Change has to come for life to struggle forward.” articula vida, change, moving-forward em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e vida.

texto: Leofgifu was skeptical, but then she always was with men. None of them could be trusted; they covered you in kisses and promises, then vanished, leaving nothing but a torn heart, and ofttimes a swelling belly.
explicacao: Citação de Helen Hollick sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “Leofgifu was skeptical, but then she always was with men. None of them could be trusted; they cov…” articula amor, men, reflexao em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

texto: Emma felt frightened, apprehensive, yes, but also elated and excited, her feelings all tumbled and mixed together like a stew of varied ingredients tossed into the same pot. She was aware of the blood coursing through her veins, the beat of her heart, the breath in her lungs. Was aware, too, of that clenched knot that hung in the pit of her stomach. This was what it was to be alive, to be at the edge, facing survival eye to eye, knowing, KNOWING, you would win.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Helen Hollick: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Emma felt frightened, apprehensive, yes, but also elated and excited, her feelings all tumbled an…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, living, mixed-emotions pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mixed-emotions e survival.

This was what it was to be alive, to be at the edge, facing survival eye to eye, knowing, KNOWING, you would win.

texto: One day, lad, your eyes will light upon a woman, and you will never forget that glint in her eye, that toss of her head, or sway of her hips. You will dream of her, whether you are asleep of awake. She will possess your mind, and your body will be on fire for her. Nothing will ever erase the linger of her scent in your nostrils, the touch of her hand on your body, the feel of her flesh beneath your fingers.When you find a woman to love, Cnut, your life changes forever.
explicacao: Citação de Helen Hollick sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “One day, lad, your eyes will light upon a woman, and you will never forget that glint in her eye,…” articula motivacao, love, women em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e women.
